body:
Trade Operations Handbook

This handbook documents the customs and trade-compliance procedures Kim Trade Services maintains for Tri-State Logistics. Owner of record: Frank Sutton.

Scope: import entry, recordkeeping, and broker coordination for all Tri-State shipments.
title: Tri-State Logistics — Trade Operations Handbook

body:
Importer of Record responsibilities

Tri-State acts as Importer of Record on covered shipments.

Maintain a valid customs power of attorney on file with Kim Trade Services.

Reasonable care obligations apply to every entry.
title: Importer of Record responsibilities

body:
Customs entry filing procedure

Submit commercial invoice, packing list, and bill of lading 48 hours before arrival.

Kim Trade Services files the CBP Form 7501 entry summary.

Confirm HTS classification with Marcus Webb before high-value entries.
title: Customs entry filing procedure

body:
ISF 10+2 filing checklist

File the Importer Security Filing no later than 24 hours before lading.

Ten importer elements plus two carrier elements required.

Late filings incur liquidated damages against the bond.
title: ISF 10+2 filing checklist

body:
Recordkeeping and retention

Retain entry records for five years from the date of entry.

Store commercial documents in the shared archive, not local drives.

Donna Schaefer audits retention quarterly.
title: Recordkeeping and retention

body:
HTS classification reference

Common Tri-State commodities and their headings are listed here.

Escalate ambiguous classifications to Priya Anand.

Document the GRI reasoning for every new classification.
title: HTS classification reference

body:
Duty payment and statements

Periodic Monthly Statement is used for duty payment.

Reconcile the statement against entries before authorizing payment.

Flag any MPF or HMF discrepancies immediately.
title: Duty payment and statements

body:
Broker coordination and escalation

Day-to-day contact is Marcus Webb at Kim Trade Services.

Escalate holds and exams to Jason Kim same day.

After-hours port issues go through the on-call line.
title: Broker coordination and escalation

body:
Bond sufficiency review

Continuous bond reviewed annually against import volume.

Insufficient bonds trigger CBP demand for a new bond.

Track the 10 percent calculation against rolling duties.
title: Bond sufficiency review

body:
Free trade agreement claims

USMCA claims require a valid certification of origin on file.

Verify originating status before claiming preferential duty.

Keep supplier origin statements for the retention period.
title: Free trade agreement claims

body:
Penalty and protest procedure

Protests must be filed within 180 days of liquidation.

Document the basis and gather supporting rulings.

Jason Kim reviews all protests before filing.
title: Penalty and protest procedure

body:
Trade Operations Handbook

This handbook documents the customs and food-import compliance procedures Kim Trade Services maintains for Maumee Valley Foods. Owner of record: Michael Donnelly.

Scope: FDA-regulated food imports, prior notice, and customs entry.
title: Maumee Valley Foods — Trade Operations Handbook

body:
FDA prior notice procedure

File prior notice for all imported food shipments.

Submit no more than 5 calendar days before arrival.

Align prior notice with the carrier ETA at the port of entry.
title: FDA prior notice procedure

body:
FSVP and supplier verification

Foreign Supplier Verification Program applies to covered foods.

Maintain supplier verification records for each foreign supplier.

Priya Anand reviews FSVP files annually.
title: FSVP and supplier verification

body:
Cold chain and frozen foods entry

Frozen food entries require temperature documentation.

Coordinate exams to avoid cold chain breaks.

Flag perishable holds to Jason Kim immediately.
title: Cold chain and frozen foods entry

body:
HTS classification for foodstuffs

Prepared and frozen foods commonly fall in Chapters 16 through 21.

Confirm added-sugar and composition for correct subheading.

Escalate novel products to Marcus Webb.
title: HTS classification for foodstuffs

body:
Customs entry filing procedure

Provide commercial invoice and packing list 48 hours before arrival.

Kim Trade Services files the entry and FDA data set together.

Confirm country of origin marking compliance.
title: Customs entry filing procedure

body:
Country of origin marking

All retail food packaging must be properly marked.

Verify marking before release to avoid redelivery notices.

Document marking decisions in the entry file.
title: Country of origin marking

body:
Recordkeeping and retention

Retain entry and FDA records for the required periods.

Store documents in the shared archive.

Donna Schaefer audits retention quarterly.
title: Recordkeeping and retention

body:
Detention and exam response

Respond to FDA detention notices within the response window.

Provide testing or relabeling evidence as required.

Jason Kim signs off on detention responses.
title: Detention and exam response

body:
Duty payment and statements

Use the Periodic Monthly Statement for duty payment.

Reconcile statements against entries before paying.

Watch for ag-specific fees and quotas.
title: Duty payment and statements

body:
Broker coordination and escalation

Primary contact is Priya Anand at Kim Trade Services.

Escalate FDA holds to Jason Kim same day.

Track open holds on the shared board.
title: Broker coordination and escalation

body:
USMCA Certification of Origin

Standard certification template used across client handbooks.

Complete all nine data elements before issuing.
title: Shared — USMCA Certification of Origin template

body:
Customs Power of Attorney

Every importer client must have a valid POA on file.

Renew before expiration; track in the compliance calendar.
title: Shared — Customs power of attorney process
